PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Sep-24-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL BROOKS CARROLL, Esq. of Law Offices of Michael Brooks Carroll (Tel: 415-788-7600) for Plaintiff/Petitioner;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MARK BELVEDERE'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION.

Matter discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Respondent's petition to vacate arbitration award is denied.

Under California law, a petition to vacate an arbitration award "shall be served and filed not later than 100 days after the date of the service of a signed copy of the award on petitioner." (Code Civ. Pro. section 1288.) The court cannot vacate an arbitration award unless a petition or response requesting that the award be vacated is "duly served and filed." (Code Civ. Pro section 1286.4.) In this case, the Corrected Arbitration Award was served on the parties on July 14, 2020. Thus, to be timely, a petition to vacate the Award needed to have been served and filed within 100 days of July 14, 2020, or October 22, 2020. Petitioner filed his petition to vacate November 2, 2020, well after the 100-day requirement. The 100-day requirement is jurisdictional, in that Petitioners failure to meet them deprives the court of an ability to grant any relief. (Abers v. Rohrs (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 1199, 1211.) The motion is denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Feb-20-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances:

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT MARK BELVEDERE's Motion To Dismiss as follows:.

Defendant Mark Belvedere's motion to dismiss is denied, on several independent grounds. First, the court stayed this action on July 14, 2011 and never lifted the stay to allow for the filing of this motion. Second, defendant fails to show that the 5-year period for a mandatory dismissal has run. The 5-year period was tolled pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 583.340(b) and (c) because the action was stayed and/or it was "impossible, impracticable, or futile" to prosecute this action while the matter was sent to arbitration. (Gaines v. Fidelity National Title Ins. Co. (2016) 62 Cal.4th 1081, 1091-1092 [a stay of proceedings tolls the five-year period under 583.340(b)].) Third, this court has no jurisdiction to dismiss the pending arbitration. "It is also up to the arbitrator, and not the court, to grant relief for delay in bringing an arbitration to a resolution." (Titan/Value Equities Group, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 482, 488; accord, Nanfito v. Superior Court (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 315, 318 ["where the superior court has compelled arbitration and stayed court proceedings pursuant to the terms of a written settlement agreement [citations], the 'arbitration has a life of its own outside the judicial system, and only the arbitrator should determine whether there has been an unreasonable delay in prosecution which would justify dismissal.'"].) "This does not mean that a party to an arbitration proceeding has no remedy against dilatory tactics. The offended party may move in the arbitration proceedings to terminate them for failure to pursue the arbitration claim with reasonable diligence." (Brock v. Kaiser Foundation Hospitals (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1790, 1808 [holding that "the trial court has no jurisdiction to dismiss an arbitration proceeding for failure to prosecute it in a reasonably diligent fashion"].) Accordingly, defendant can renew his motion to dismiss before the arbitrator.

Counsel for Plaintiff is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
